definitions: Containing an amount of a substance greater than that which is normally possible to dissolve at a given temperature.
Having been supplied with an excessive amount of something, often to the point of being detrimental or ineffective.
Having colors that are excessively intense or vivid, often to an unnatural degree.
examples: The market is oversaturated with similar products.
The image was oversaturated, making the colors look artificial.
An oversaturated solution can be unstable.
